data.title: Valaris, Halliburton in pact to develop Petronas assets offshore Suriname
data.description:
Valaris (VAL) and Halliburton (HAL) said Monday they entered into a strategic collaboration agreement with Malaysia's oil and gas company Petronas to support the development of Suriname's offshore assets.

The collaboration combines Valaris' (VAL) offshore drilling expertise and high-specification global fleet, Petronas' project stewardship and basin knowledge, and Halliburton's (HAL) subsurface, well construction, and digital solutions.

Suriname is part of the Guyana-Suriname Basin, which continues to see strong exploration success and development activity and offers long-term opportunities for operators and service providers.

Separately, Valaris (VAL) said it was awarded a 1,064-day contract extension with Petrobras (PBR) offshore Brazil for the Valaris DS-4 drillship beginning November 2027 in direct continuation of the existing program, adding ~$447M to contract backlog.

data.title: AppLovin mobile ad dominance is 'real,' but risks evolve: Wedbush
data.description:
AppLovin's (APP) presence in mobile advertising is still exceptionally strong, based on the prowess of its MAX, Adjust, and AXON platforms, Wedbush Securities said. However, there does appear to be rising competition, the investment firm added. 

“AppLovin commands the largest share of mobile gaming ad spend by a wide margin, and its walled garden — built on MAX, Adjust, and AXON — remains the strongest in the ecosystem,” analyst Alicia Reese wrote in a note to clients. “That said, our expert noted a few dynamics worth monitoring: Meta is reclaiming some iOS share of voice after years of post-ATT decline. While individual studio spend with AppLovin is moderating as pricing rises, AppLovin's overall footprint continues to expand as it onboards more partners, suggesting the growth engine could shift from deepening wallet share to greater breadth of relationships.”

Delving deeper, Reese said the underlying architectures of AppLovin's AXON and Unity's (U) Vector are “fundamentally similar,” but the difference comes in first-party data. “Unity's upcoming Runtime integration — which would pipe in granular, game-level telemetry including session behavior, churn points, and ad disruption data — is the most important catalyst for closing the gap,” Reese added. “Unity should begin ingesting some of this data into models this year.”

Additionally, Unity is attempting to turn itself around with new management, and it appears to be making some headway on that front, Reese added.

“The decision to deprecate ironSource signaled internal confidence rather than desperation,” Reese wrote. “The organizational shift to a less-is-more philosophy is freeing resources for the core ad network. Our expert's view was that if Unity's one-stop-shop vision (engine + monetization + Runtime data) comes together, the company has 'as big an opportunity as anyone.'”

Reese also pointed to Meta Platforms (META) as a competitor in gaming, as its iOS share of voice in gaming has climbed “meaningfully” over the past six months. 

“Both AppLovin and Unity are aware of this shift, though neither appears to be aggressively defending against it yet, as market-share loss has likely been minimal and has not materially impeded growth for either,” Reese added. “Meta doesn't currently offer a mediation platform, but our expert noted significant publisher appetite to test one if launched.”

data.title: Oracle appoints Hilary Maxson as CFO
data.description:
• Oracle (NYSE: ORCL) on Monday said it has appointed Hilary Maxson as chief financial officer, effective April 6, reporting to CEO Clay Magouyrk.
  • Maxson joins from Schneider Electric, where she served as Executive VP and group CFO, and will lead Oracle’s global finance organization.
  • She succeeds Doug Kehring, who had been serving in the role and will transition to focus on go-to-market operations, the company said.
  • ORCL +0.36% premarket to $146.92.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Expand Energy taps Marcel Teunissen as CFO
data.description:
• Expand Energy (EXE) has appointed Marcel Teunissen as chief financial officer, effective April 6, 2026.
  • Teunissen most recently served as president, North America for Parkland Corporation. Prior to Parkland, Teunissen spent more than 20 years with Shell in senior and executive finance, commercial, and strategy roles across upstream and integrated gas businesses.

data.title: Stereotaxis wins FDA clearance for Synchrony system, launches platform
data.description:
• Stereotaxis (STXS) on Monday said it has received U.S. Food and Drug Administration 510(k) clearance for its Synchrony system and has begun its commercial launch
  • The company said the system is designed to modernize interventional catheterization labs by integrating display, control, and workflow functions, and is paired with its cloud-based SynX application for remote connectivity and collaboration.
  • Stereotaxis reiterated expectations of more than $3 million in revenue this year from Synchrony systems placed independently of its robotic platforms.
  • STXS +6.95% premarket to $2.0.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Old Republic launches new company to underwrite specialized property insurance products
data.description:
Old Republic International (ORI) on Monday said it is forming a new operating company, Old Republic Property, for specialized property insurance products.

The products' underwriting will be made through a national retail broker distribution network.

"Property insurance is a core line that aligns well with Old Republic's long-standing strategy and focus on diversified growth in specialty insurance," said CEO Craig Smiddy.

data.title: Molson Coors looks outside of beer for stronger growth
data.description:
Molson Coors Beverage Company (TAP) closed on its acquisition of Atomic Brands in a development that brings Monaco Cocktails into the company's U.S. Beyond Beer portfolio.

Weighing in on the deal, TD Cowen analyst Robert Moskow noted that while Monaco's growth has slowed in the past year, it is still considered a strong brand in the rapidly evolving RTD spirits category. Moskow and his team anticipate a 1% lift to Molson Coors' (TAP) revenue from the acquisition. Evercore ISI was also positive on the deal. Analyst Robert Ottenstein said the addition of higher-growth brands helps Molson Coors (TAP) work toward returning to delivering results in line with its low single-digit, medium-term sales algorithm, which is now inclusive of M&A. "Monaco fits well given the distribution overlap and strength in independents and c-stores, while runway remains as the brand starts to expand into large format and as on-premise venues become a bigger priority," updated Ottenstein.

The acquisition was noted to establish Molson Coors (ATP) as a top-five supplier in the fast-growing ready-to-drink cocktail segment. Looking ahead, Molson Coors (TAP) is focused on supporting the plan for Monaco's next phase of growth and leveraging its national scale while maintaining continuity for customers, distributors, and consumers. The acquisition is also expected to support the company’s long-term strategy to build a strong portfolio of scaled brands across beer and beyond beer, aligned with evolving consumer preferences and occasions.

Launched in 2012 by Atomic Brands, Monaco helped ignite the RTD cocktail category and popularized the concept of canned cocktails for big nights, high-intensity sports, and live events. Monaco Cocktails was introduced as Atomic's flagship ready-to-drink (RTD) line, positioned around "premium quality, bold flavor, endless energy, plus zero fuss."

Monaco gradually broadened its base formulas beyond pure spirits to include wine- and malt-based versions, allowing distribution and pricing flexibility while still highlighting flavor and strength. Since the initial launch, Monaco has grown to become a top-five RTD cocktail brand* in the U.S. and holds a 5% market share of RTD singles, in addition to now being the top independently owned RTD singles cocktail brand in the U.S. across all tracked retail channels. Notably, Monaco Cocktails is sold in over 70K retail locations across the U.S. and shows up particularly strongly in convenience stores.

data.title: Twilio rises after Jefferies upgrades to Buy
data.description:
Shares of Twilio (TWLO) climbed about 3% premarket on Monday after Jefferies upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold.

The firm raised the price target to $160 from $125 on the shares of Twilio — which provides application programming interfaces, or APIs, and software solutions for communication between customers and end users.

"We are upgrading TWLO to Buy as we have greater conviction in the role TWLO will play in the Voice AI tech stack. Further, even a modest increase in traction can serve as a sustainable tailwind for GP$ [gross profit] growth for years to come and drive upside to consensus," said analyst Samad Samana.

The analyst believes that the combination of greater relevance in an agentic AI world plus better fundamentals will result in a better valuation multiple as well.

Samana noted that voice AI represents the convergence of cloud telephony, real-time media streaming, speech-to-text, or STT, large language model, or LLM, inference, and text-to-speech, or TTS, to enable fully automated, conversational call interactions. The analyst views voice AI adoption as a structural volume tailwind for the company, with value creation occurring across the stack but monetization concentrated at the orchestration layer.

data.title: Riley Gold raises C$1.67M from warrant exercises
data.description:
• Riley Gold (RLYG:CA) (OTCQB: RLYGF) on Monday said it has received about C$1.67 million in gross proceeds from the exercise of 6.68 million warrants tied to its April 2024 private placement.
  • The company said the exercised warrants represented about 86% of those outstanding before their April 2, 2026 expiry, signaling investor participation.
  • Riley Gold added that CEO Todd Hilditch increased his stake to about 10.13% of outstanding shares following the exercise of warrants held through a controlled entity.
  • KGC +0.72% premarket to $31.74.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hillman Solutions buys Campbell Chain & Fittings
data.description:
• Hillman Solutions (HLMN) has acquired the Campbell Chain & Fittings business, a manufacturer and supplier of industrial chain and chain-related products, from Apex Tool Group.
  • Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed. Hillman expects to recognize over $20M in net sales during its 2026 fiscal year from the Campbell acquisition.
  • This acquisition expands Hillman’s position within the industrial MRO sector of the market, expanding Hillman’s breadth of chain offerings to include commercial and industrial applications, adding grade 70, grade 80, and grade 100 chain to its product portfolio.
